data.content:
### Sandbox Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Methods for creating new sandbox instances, either from a default template or a specified one. Includes beta creation options.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Creation API

### `create()`

#### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance. It can either use the default 'base' sandbox template or a specified template.

#### Method
`POST`

#### Endpoint
`/sandboxes`

#### Parameters

##### Request Body
- **template** (string, Optional) - The name or ID of the sandbox template to use. If not provided, the default 'base' template is used.
- **opts** (object, Optional) - Connection options for the sandbox.
  - **requestTimeoutMs** (number, Optional) - Timeout in milliseconds for requests.

#### Request Example
```json
{
  "template": "my-custom-template",
  "opts": {
    "requestTimeoutMs": 60000
  }
}
```

#### Response

##### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxId** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox.
- **instance** (object) - The sandbox instance object.

##### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox_12345",
  "instance": {}
}
```

### `betaCreate()`

#### Description
**`Beta`** - Creates a new sandbox instance using a beta feature. Similar to `create()`, it can use a default or specified template.

#### Method
`POST`

#### Endpoint
`/sandboxes/beta`

#### Parameters

##### Request Body
- **template** (string, Optional) - The name or ID of the sandbox template to use. If not provided, the default 'base' template is used.
- **opts** (object, Optional) - Connection options for the sandbox.
  - **requestTimeoutMs** (number, Optional) - Timeout in milliseconds for requests.

#### Request Example
```json
{
  "template": "beta-template",
  "opts": {
    "requestTimeoutMs": 120000
  }
}
```

#### Response

##### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxId** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox.
- **instance** (object) - The sandbox instance object.

##### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox_beta_67890",
  "instance": {}
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Create new sandboxes. Supports creation from a default template or a specified template.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/sandboxes

### Description
Create a new sandbox. You can create a sandbox from the default template or a specified template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/v1/sandboxes`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **template** (string) - Optional - The name or ID of the sandbox template to use. If not provided, the default template is used.
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional - Connection options for the new sandbox.

### Request Example
**Default Template:**
```json
{
  "opts": {
    "accessToken": "string",
    "apiKey": "string",
    "debug": "boolean",
    "domain": "string",
    "envs": { "key": "value" },
    "headers": { "key": "value" }
  }
}
```
**Specified Template:**
```json
{
  "template": "<template-name-or-id>",
  "opts": {
    "accessToken": "string",
    "apiKey": "string",
    "debug": "boolean",
    "domain": "string",
    "envs": { "key": "value" },
    "headers": { "key": "value" }
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox** (InstanceType<S>) - The newly created sandbox instance.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox": "SandboxInstance"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.3/sandbox/page.mdx

Use Sandbox.create to create a new, isolated cloud sandbox environment.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Creation

### Description
Creates a new, isolated cloud sandbox environment. The sandbox provides access to a Linux OS, file system operations, command execution, and internet access.

### Method
`static create()`

### Endpoint
N/A (Static method)

### Parameters
None

### Request Example
```ts
import { Sandbox } from 'e2b'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

### Response
#### Success Response
- `sandbox` (Sandbox) - An instance of the created Sandbox object.

#### Response Example
```typescript
// Example sandbox object structure (simplified)
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox_abc123",
  "commands": { ... },
  "files": { ... },
  "pty": { ... }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/sandbox/page.mdx

API endpoint for creating new sandboxes.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandbox

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance from the default base template. The sandbox is a secure and isolated cloud environment.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandbox

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional - Options for creating the sandbox, such as timeout and metadata.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "timeoutMs": 3600000, 
  "metadata": {
    "userId": "user123"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxId** (string) - The unique identifier of the created sandbox.
- **host** (string) - The host address for connecting to the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sbx_abcdef123456",
  "host": "http://localhost:8080"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/sandbox/page.mdx

This section details how to create a new sandbox instance using the `create` method.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandbox/create

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandbox/create

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional - Options for creating the sandbox.

### Request Example
```typescript
import { Sandbox } from 'e2b'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxInstance** (InstanceType<S>) - The created sandbox instance.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox-12345"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Provides instructions on how to create a new sandbox instance using the Sandbox.create() method.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/sandboxes

### Description
Creates a new isolated cloud sandbox environment.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/sandboxes

### Request Body
- **template** (string, Optional) - Specifies the sandbox template to use. Defaults to 'ubuntu-22.04'.
- **cmd** (string, Optional) - The initial command to run in the sandbox.
- **metadata** (object, Optional) - Key-value pairs for sandbox metadata.
- **env_vars** (object, Optional) - Environment variables to set in the sandbox.
- **secrets** (object, Optional) - Secrets to be available in the sandbox.
- **timeout_ms** (number, Optional) - The maximum time in milliseconds the sandbox can run. Maximum 24 hours (86,400,000 ms) for Pro users and 1 hour (3,600,000 ms) for Hobby users.
- **idle_timeout_ms** (number, Optional) - The maximum time in milliseconds the sandbox can remain idle before being automatically killed.
- **cpu_limit** (number, Optional) - CPU limit for the sandbox in millicores (e.g., 1000 for 1 vCPU).
- **memory_limit** (number, Optional) - Memory limit for the sandbox in megabytes (e.g., 2048 for 2GB).
- **disk_limit** (number, Optional) - Disk limit for the sandbox in megabytes (e.g., 10240 for 10GB).

### Request Example
```typescript
import { Sandbox } from 'e2b'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create({
  template: 'ubuntu-22.04',
  cmd: 'sleep infinity',
  timeout_ms: 3600000 // 1 hour
});
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxId** (string) - The unique identifier of the created sandbox.
- **sandboxDomain** (string) - The domain where the sandbox is hosted.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox-12345",
  "sandboxDomain": "sandbox-12345.e2b.dev"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Provides an example of how to create a new sandbox environment using the `Sandbox.create` method.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/sandboxes (or equivalent)

### Description
Creates a new, isolated cloud sandbox environment. This sandbox provides a secure Linux OS for running commands, managing files, and executing code.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/api/sandboxes` (This is a conceptual endpoint as the actual SDK call is `Sandbox.create()`)

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional - Options for creating the sandbox, such as `base`, `timeoutMs`, `metadata`, `envs`.

### Request Example
```ts
import { Sandbox } from 'e2b'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxId** (string) - The unique identifier of the newly created sandbox.
- **instance** (Sandbox) - An instance of the created sandbox object, providing access to its methods and properties.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox_abc123",
  "instance": { ... sandbox object ... }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.3/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Allows for the creation of new sandboxes with various configuration options.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes

### Description
Create a new sandbox. By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **template** (string) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID
- **timeout** (integer) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. The maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.
- **auto_pause** (boolean) - Optional - Automatically pause the sandbox after the timeout expires. Defaults to `False`.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox. Example: `{"key": "value"}`
- **envs** (object) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox. Example: `{"key": "value"}
- **secure** (boolean) - Optional - Envd is secured with access token and cannot be used without it, defaults to `True`.
- **allow_internet_access** (boolean) - Optional - Allow sandbox to access the internet, defaults to `True`.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "template": "my-template-id",
  "timeout": 600,
  "auto_pause": true,
  "metadata": {"user_id": "123"},
  "envs": {"API_KEY": "abc"},
  "secure": false,
  "allow_internet_access": false
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (201 Created)
- **sandboxId** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox.
- **sandboxUrl** (string) - The URL to access the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox-12345",
  "sandboxUrl": "https://sandbox.e2b.dev/sandbox-12345"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.0.4/sandbox/page.mdx

This section covers the creation of a new sandbox environment using the `Sandbox.create()` method.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Creation

### Description
Creates a new, isolated cloud sandbox environment.

### Method
`Sandbox.create()`

### Endpoint
N/A (Client-side SDK method)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```ts
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter';

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create();
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox** (object) - An object representing the created sandbox instance.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sandbox_abc123"
}
```
```
